name: Dagbani
notes: STUB: the grapheme inventory of Dagbani is NOT yet specified (graphemes/allophones are empty). Dagbani (Dagbanli) is a Gur (Atlantic-Congo) language of northern Ghana, written in the Latin script with Africa-Alphabet letters (ɛ ɔ ŋ ʒ) and digraphs; it is tonal, and tone is not marked in ordinary orthography. INPUT CONTRACT: Latin-script Dagbani orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode dagb1246), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode dagb1246.

ancestors.notes: Atlantic-Congo — the classification node this language sits under.
